FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class TextUndoStack

Namespace
FluentGpu.Text
Assembly
FluentGpu.Text.dll

Bounded undo/redo for the text editor (cap 512 ops, oldest dropped — a hard memory ceiling per field, like RichEdit). Coalescing makes one undo step out of a typing burst: a new op merges into the open top op when the kinds match AND the positions are contiguous (typing extends forward from the previous insert's end; backspace walks backward, forward-delete stays in place) AND no selection was replaced AND the caller did not seal. Paste, IME commit, selection-replacing edits, and any caret relocation seal the run (SealRun() or sealRun: true on Push(in TextUndoOp, bool)) so they become their own undo steps — the WinUI/RichEdit feel: "abc" undoes in one step, a paste in its own step.

public sealed class TextUndoStack
Inheritance
object
TextUndoStack

object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Fields

MaxOps

Maximum retained undo ops; pushing past it drops the oldest.

public const int MaxOps = 512

Field Value

int

Properties

CanRedo 

public bool CanRedo { get; }

Property Value

bool

CanUndo 

public bool CanUndo { get; }

Property Value

bool

RedoDepth 

public int RedoDepth { get; }

Property Value

int

UndoDepth

Observable op counts (headless checks assert coalescing — e.g. typing "abc" leaves depth 1).

public int UndoDepth { get; }

Property Value

int

Methods

Clear()

Drop all history (programmatic text reset — WinUI clears undo on a Text set).

public void Clear()

Push(in TextUndoOp, bool)

Record an applied edit. Clears the redo stack (a fresh edit forks history). When sealRun is false the op may coalesce into the open top op per the kind rules above; when true it neither merges nor accepts future merges (paste/IME-commit/selection-replace semantics).

public void Push(in TextUndoOp op, bool sealRun = false)

Parameters

op TextUndoOp
sealRun bool

SealRun()

End the current coalescing run: the next push starts a fresh undo step. Called on caret moves, selection changes, focus changes, and around paste/IME commits.

public void SealRun()

TryRedo(out TextUndoOp)

Pop the newest undone op for the caller to re-apply; it moves back to the undo stack (sealed).

public bool TryRedo(out TextUndoOp op)

Parameters

op TextUndoOp

Returns

bool

TryUndo(out TextUndoOp)

Pop the newest op for the caller to reverse; it moves to the redo stack. The surviving top can no longer coalesce (undoing then typing must not mutate history that was already stepped over).

public bool TryUndo(out TextUndoOp op)

Parameters

op TextUndoOp

Returns

bool
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
